page: Vultr voc-g-4c-16gb-80s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring an x86_64 AMD EPYC processor with 4 shared vCPUs, derived from 2 physical cores and 2 threads per core. Operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz, the CPU includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. The hardware configuration is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB per core, and includes 80 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate a strong single-core profile alongside average multi-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU test places this server in the top tier, while multi-core performance falls into the average category. Memory bandwidth tests show average read speeds but strong large-block write speeds. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks perform strongly, though multi-threaded decompression is relatively weak. Database operations and static web serving throughput also rank in the lower tier, while Redis SET operations maintain average performance.

Given its shared CPU allocation, this instance offers a cost-effective entry point for workloads that require high single-core bursts but do not demand sustained, dedicated multi-core execution. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it suitable for general-purpose applications, lightweight microservices, development environments, and basic caching layers. However, the shared CPU model and lower multi-threaded decompression and database benchmark scores represent a tradeoff for sustained enterprise database hosting or heavy multi-threaded processing.
description: Vultr voc-g-4c-16gb-80s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance powered by an x86_64 AMD EPYC processor with 4 shared vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and a 2.0 GHz clock speed. It features 16.0 GB of RAM and 80 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong memory write bandwidth, though multi-core performance and database operations remain average to weak. The shared CPU allocation provides a cost-effective resource profile for general-purpose workloads, development environments, and lightweight microservices. However, the shared CPU model and lower multi-threaded performance make it less suitable for intensive database hosting or sustained multi-threaded computing.
og_description: Vultr voc-g-4c-16gb-80s-amd is an AMD EPYC-powered instance with 4 shared vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 80 GB NVMe SSD storage, offering strong single-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-g-4c-16gb-80s-amd is a shared AMD EPYC instance with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 80 GB NVMe storage for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A shared AMD EPYC instance balancing strong single-core performance with NVMe storage for general-purpose and development workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC processor with 4 shared vCPUs and 2.0 GHz clock speed
16.0 GB system memory and 80 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Strong memory write bandwidth and single-threaded compression
Best suited for general-purpose applications, microservices, and development environments
